title: North Korea Tests SRBM Cluster Warheads as Party Congress Mandates Expanded Conventional Strike Capability
summary: North Korea tested KN-23 and Hwasong-11D short-range ballistic missiles with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads in April, demonstrating submunition payloads that Kim Jong Un ordered for mass production.
contents:
## DPRK Demonstrates Cluster Warhead Capability on Short-Range Missiles

### April Test Series
North Korea conducted two rounds of SRBM tests in April featuring cluster warhead payloads. On April 6-8, a KN-23/Hwasong-11A missile tested a "cluster bomb warhead" covering 6.5-7 hectares with "highest-density power." On April 19, five improved Hwasong-11D missiles tested both cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads, covering 12.5-13 hectares.

### Kim Jong Un's Directive
Kim Jong Un personally supervised the April 19 test and stated that "different cluster bomb warheads can meet the operational demand of the KPA in a more satisfactory and effective way" to boost high-density striking capability. A specialized warhead research group developed the warheads over five years.

### Strategic Significance
38 North analysts assessed these tests underscore the important conventional warfighting roles of North Korea's SRBM force, which compensates for long-standing allied air superiority on the Korean Peninsula. The tests also fulfill the February 2026 Ninth Party Congress mandate to reinforce SRBM deployments.

### Iran Connection
Analysts noted North Korea likely capitalized on global attention to Iran's recent use of submunition warheads during the US-Israeli campaign to publicize similar capabilities. North Korea's missile technology transfers to Iran span over 40 years.

**Sources:** 38 North, KCNA via state media reports, Korean Central News Agency
